CITY OF 
SERSIDE 
PO BOX 157,440 HARCOURT AVE. 
Legal Division 
CALIFORNIA 93955 
TEL. 
November 12, 1982 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Post Office Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
95804 
Re: 
City of Seaside 
Gentlemen: 
On November 2, 1982, a new mayor and council member were 
elected in the City of Seaside. 
The new mayor is employed by the 
County of Monterey as an investigator in the Public Defender's 
Office. 
The councilman is employed by the Monterey County Sheriff's 
Department as a bailiff. 
At present, both are entitled to receive 
compensation from the City for their services as elected officials. 
The City has several contractual agreements with the County 
and shall likely have others in the future. 
In reference to the 
Political Reform Act of 1974 and Section 1090 et seq of the 
California Government Code, I would appreciate your assistance 
in rendering a legal opinion as to whether either of these persons 
may have potential conflicts of interest. 
It is the desire of 
the two that they avoid any conflict of interest yet be able to 
vote on City -
County agreements. 
If they elect to not take the 
stipends provided for by law, would this serve to avoid a potential 
conflict of interest? 
The City will provide you with a listing of current City -
County contracts if this would be helpful to you. 
Should you need 
further information or have any questions, please contact me at 
(408) 899-6206. 
Any suggestions you can offer will be appreciated. 
Sincerely, 
DONALD G. FREEMAN 
DGF:caj

sections.question: In reference to the Political Reform Act of 1974 and Section 1090 et seq of the California Government Code, whether either of these persons may have potential conflicts of interest. It is the desire of the two that they avoid any conflict of interest yet be able to vote on City-County agreements. If they elect to not take the stipends provided for by law, would this serve to avoid a potential conflict of interest?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a newly elected mayor employed by Monterey County as an investigator and a councilman employed by the Sheriff's Department may have conflicts of interest under Section 1090 when voting on City-County contracts, and whether declining compensation would eliminate such conflicts.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC has received the request and indicates it will provide a response within 21 working days, referencing Section 1090 of the Government Code regarding potential conflicts of interest for public officials with concurrent county employment.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (from City Attorney dated November 12, 1982) and the FPPC's acknowledgment/receipt letter (dated November 17, 1982). The full FPPC advice conclusion is not included in this document - only the transmittal letter confirming receipt and promising response within 21 days. Heavy OCR degradation in some sections reduced confidence slightly.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: In reference to the Political Reform Act of 1974 and Section 1090 et seq of the California Government Code, whether either of these persons may have potential conflicts of interest. It is the desire of the two that they avoid any conflict of interest yet be able to vote on City-County agreements. If they elect to not take the stipends provided for by law, would this serve to avoid a potential conflict of interest?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC has received the request and indicates it will provide a response within 21 working days, referencing Section 1090 of the Government Code regarding potential conflicts of interest for public officials with concurrent county employment.

The new mayor is employed by the County of Monterey as an investigator in the Public Defender's Office. The councilman is employed by the Monterey County Sheriff's Department as a bailiff. At present, both are entitled to receive compensation from the City for their services as elected officials. The City has several contractual agreements with the County and shall likely have others in the future. In reference to the Political Reform Act of 1974 and Section 1090 et seq of the California Government Code, I would appreciate your assistance in rendering a legal opinion as to whether either of these persons may have potential conflicts of interest. embedding.summary: The City Attorney of Seaside requests FPPC advice regarding potential conflicts of interest for a newly elected mayor and councilman who are employed by Monterey County, asking whether they can vote on City-County agreements and whether declining compensation would resolve conflicts under the Political Reform Act.
